016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.ops;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
021
022import java.io.*;
023import java.lang.management.*;
024import java.util.*;
025
026import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.http.response.*;
028import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.guard.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.guard.RateLimitGuard.*;
032
033/**
034 * Flavor-neutral operational-introspection worker bean shared by the admin {@code @Rest} flavors.
035 *
036 * <p>
037 * Holds the capability state (the name-keyed cache-flush hooks + the thread-name-prefix exclude list)
038 * and the serve logic for the four admin endpoints (thread dump, heap statistics, cache flush, and
039 * rate-limit-bucket inspection). The {@link AdminMixin} (mixin), {@link AdminResource} (child), and
040 * {@link AdminServlet} (servlet) flavors are independent {@code @Rest} classes that each hold an
041 * {@code AdminProvider} worker and delegate to it &mdash; so the three deployment forms cannot drift, and no
042 * flavor is another flavor's worker.
043 *
044 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
045 *    <li class='jc'>{@link AdminMixin}
046 *    <li class='jc'>{@link AdminResource}
047 *    <li class='jc'>{@link AdminServlet}
048 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerComposition">REST Server &mdash; Composition (mixins, paths)</a>
049 * </ul>
050 *
051 * @since 10.0.0
052 */
053// @formatter:off
054public class AdminProvider {
055
056   /**
057    * Default thread-name-prefix exclude list: filter out JVM internals, servlet container, and
058    * Spring Boot infrastructure threads.
059    */
060   public static final List<String> DEFAULT_THREAD_NAME_PREFIX_EXCLUDE = List.of(
061      "Reference Handler", "Finalizer", "Signal Dispatcher", "Common-Cleaner",
062      "Notification Thread", "Attach Listener",
063      "jetty-", "qtp",
064      "spring-",
065      "GC ", "G1 ");
066
067   /**
068    * Creates a new builder.
069    *
070    * @return A new builder.
071    */
072   public static Builder create() {
073      return new Builder();
074   }
075
076   private final Map<String,Runnable> cacheFlushHooks;
077   private final List<String> threadNamePrefixExclude;
078
079   /** No-arg constructor &mdash; uses the default thread filter and no cache-flush hooks. */
080   public AdminProvider() {
081      this(create());
082   }
083
084   /**
085    * Builder constructor.
086    *
087    * @param builder The builder.
088    */
089   protected AdminProvider(Builder builder) {
090      cacheFlushHooks = Collections.unmodifiableMap(new LinkedHashMap<>(builder.cacheFlushHooks));
091      threadNamePrefixExclude = List.copyOf(builder.threadNamePrefixExclude);
092   }
093
094   /**
095    * Serves the thread dump as a JSON list of currently-live threads.
096    *
097    * @param res The current REST response.
098    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs while writing the response.
099    */
100   @SuppressWarnings({
101      "java:S1874" // Thread.getId() is not deprecated on the Java 17 baseline; its replacement Thread.threadId() was only added in Java 19, so getId() is the correct call here.
102   })
103   public void serveThreads(RestResponse res) throws IOException {
104      var allTraces = Thread.getAllStackTraces();
105      var out = new ArrayList<Map<String,Object>>();
106      for (var e : allTraces.entrySet()) {
107         var t = e.getKey();
108         if (isExcludedThread(t.getName()))
109            continue;
110         var entry = new LinkedHashMap<String,Object>();
111         entry.put("name", t.getName());
112         entry.put("id", t.getId());
113         entry.put("state", t.getState().toString());
114         entry.put("daemon", t.isDaemon());
115         entry.put("priority", t.getPriority());
116         var stack = new ArrayList<String>();
117         for (var f : e.getValue())
118            stack.add(f.toString());
119         entry.put("stack", stack);
120         out.add(entry);
121      }
122      writeJson(res, out);
123   }
124
125   /**
126    * Serves JVM heap and non-heap memory statistics.
127    *
128    * @param res The current REST response.
129    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs while writing the response.
130    */
131   public void serveHeap(RestResponse res) throws IOException {
132      var rt = Runtime.getRuntime();
133      var heap = new LinkedHashMap<String,Object>();
134      heap.put("total", rt.totalMemory());
135      heap.put("free", rt.freeMemory());
136      heap.put("max", rt.maxMemory());
137      heap.put("used", rt.totalMemory() - rt.freeMemory());
138
139      var mx = ManagementFactory.getMemoryMXBean();
140      var nonHeapUsage = mx.getNonHeapMemoryUsage();
141      var nonHeap = new LinkedHashMap<String,Object>();
142      nonHeap.put("init", nonHeapUsage.getInit());
143      nonHeap.put("used", nonHeapUsage.getUsed());
144      nonHeap.put("committed", nonHeapUsage.getCommitted());
145      nonHeap.put("max", nonHeapUsage.getMax());
146
147      var out = new LinkedHashMap<String,Object>();
148      out.put("heap", heap);
149      out.put("nonHeap", nonHeap);
150      out.put("availableProcessors", rt.availableProcessors());
151      writeJson(res, out);
152   }
153
154   /**
155    * Runs all registered cache-flush hooks (or a subset).
156    *
157    * <p>
158    * When {@code names} is supplied, only the named hooks are invoked. Unknown names are
159    * silently ignored (404-on-unknown would leak the registered hook set). Hook execution is
160    * synchronous; long-running hooks block the request thread.
161    *
162    * @param req The current REST request &mdash; {@code names} query parameter is read off it.
163    * @param res The current REST response.
164    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs while writing the response.
165    */
166   public void serveCacheFlush(RestRequest req, RestResponse res) throws IOException {
167      var namesParam = req.getQueryParams().get("names").asString().orElse(null);
168      Set<String> selected = null;
169      if (namesParam != null && ! namesParam.isBlank()) {
170         selected = new LinkedHashSet<>();
171         for (var n : namesParam.split(","))
172            if (! n.isBlank())
173               selected.add(n.trim());
174      }
175      var executed = new ArrayList<String>();
176      for (var e : cacheFlushHooks.entrySet()) {
177         if (selected != null && ! selected.contains(e.getKey()))
178            continue;
179         e.getValue().run();
180         executed.add(e.getKey());
181      }
182      var out = new LinkedHashMap<String,Object>();
183      out.put("registered", new ArrayList<>(cacheFlushHooks.keySet()));
184      out.put("executed", executed);
185      writeJson(res, out);
186   }
187
188   /**
189    * Serves the registered {@link RateLimitGuard} bean configuration.
190    *
191    * <p>
192    * Returns {@code 404 Not Found} when no {@code RateLimitGuard} bean is registered.
193    *
194    * @param req The current REST request &mdash; supplies the bean store for guard lookup.
195    * @param res The current REST response.
196    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs while writing the response.
197    * @throws NotFound If no {@link RateLimitGuard} bean is registered on the importer's bean store.
198    */
199   public void serveRateLimit(RestRequest req, RestResponse res) throws IOException {
200      var bs = req.getContext().getBeanStore();
201      var guards = collectRateLimitGuards(bs);
202      if (guards.isEmpty())
203         throw new NotFound("No RateLimitGuard bean is registered.");
204      var entries = new LinkedHashMap<String,Object>();
205      for (var e : guards.entrySet()) {
206         var g = e.getValue();
207         var bucket = new LinkedHashMap<String,Object>();
208         bucket.put("config", describeRateLimitGuard(g));
209         bucket.put("snapshot", g.snapshot().values().stream()
210            .sorted(Comparator.comparing(BucketState::key))
211            .toList());
212         entries.put(e.getKey(), bucket);
213      }
214      var out = new LinkedHashMap<String,Object>();
215      out.put("guards", entries);
216      writeJson(res, out);
217   }
218
219   /**
220    * Returns the registered cache-flush hooks (test/inspection helper).
221    *
222    * @return The hooks, keyed by registration name. Never {@code null}.
223    */
224   public Map<String,Runnable> getCacheFlushHooks() {
225      return cacheFlushHooks;
226   }
227
228   /**
229    * Returns the configured thread-name-prefix exclude list (test/inspection helper).
230    *
231    * @return The exclude list. Never {@code null}.
232    */
233   public List<String> getThreadNamePrefixExclude() {
234      return threadNamePrefixExclude;
235   }
236
237   private boolean isExcludedThread(String threadName) {
238      for (var prefix : threadNamePrefixExclude)
239         if (threadName != null && threadName.startsWith(prefix))
240            return true;
241      return false;
242   }
243
244   private static Map<String,RateLimitGuard> collectRateLimitGuards(BeanStore bs) {
245      // Multi-bean lookup so users running per-tier guards (free / paid / etc.) see every
246      // registered bean keyed by its bean name. Falls through to the single-bean path when no
247      // per-tier configuration is registered.
248      var byName = bs.getBeansOfType(RateLimitGuard.class);
249      if (byName != null && ! byName.isEmpty())
250         return new LinkedHashMap<>(byName);
251      var out = new LinkedHashMap<String,RateLimitGuard>();
252      bs.getBean(RateLimitGuard.class).ifPresent(g -> out.put("rateLimit", g));
253      return out;
254   }
255
256   private static Map<String,Object> describeRateLimitGuard(RateLimitGuard g) {
257      var m = new LinkedHashMap<String,Object>();
258      m.put("class", cn(g));
259      m.put("limit", g.getCapacity());
260      m.put("permitsPerSecond", g.getPermitsPerSecond());
261      m.put("xForwardedForAware", g.isXForwardedForAware());
262      m.put("exemptPaths", g.getExemptPaths().stream().sorted().toList());
263      return m;
264   }
265
266   private static void writeJson(RestResponse res, Object payload) throws IOException {
267      try (var w = res.getDirectWriter("application/json")) {
268         JsonSerializer.DEFAULT_READABLE.serialize(payload, w);
269      }
270   }
271
272   /**
273    * Builder for {@link AdminProvider} instances.
274    */
275   public static class Builder {
276
277      private final Map<String,Runnable> cacheFlushHooks = new LinkedHashMap<>();
278      private final List<String> threadNamePrefixExclude = new ArrayList<>(DEFAULT_THREAD_NAME_PREFIX_EXCLUDE);
279
280      /** Constructor &mdash; package access for {@link AdminProvider#create()}. */
281      protected Builder() {}
282
283      /**
284       * Registers a cache-flush hook under a name.
285       *
286       * <p>
287       * The hook is invoked synchronously when {@code POST /admin/cache/flush} is called (with
288       * no {@code names} parameter, or with a {@code names} parameter that includes this name).
289       *
290       * @param name The hook name. Must not be {@code null} or blank.
291       * @param hook The hook. Must not be {@code null}.
292       * @return This object.
293       */
294      public Builder cacheFlush(String name, Runnable hook) {
295         if (isBlank(name))
296            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Argument 'name' must not be null or blank");
297         if (hook == null)
298            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Argument 'hook' must not be null");
299         cacheFlushHooks.put(name, hook);
300         return this;
301      }
302
303      /**
304       * Registers multiple cache-flush hooks at once.
305       *
306       * @param hooks The hooks, keyed by registration name.
307       * @return This object.
308       */
309      public Builder cacheFlushAll(Map<String,Runnable> hooks) {
310         if (hooks != null)
311            hooks.forEach(this::cacheFlush);
312         return this;
313      }
314
315      /**
316       * Replaces the thread-name-prefix exclude list.
317       *
318       * <p>
319       * Threads whose {@link Thread#getName() name} starts with any of the supplied prefixes are
320       * omitted from the {@code /admin/threads} output. Pass an empty array to disable
321       * filtering entirely.
322       *
323       * @param values The thread-name prefixes to exclude. Must not be {@code null}.
324       * @return This object.
325       */
326      public Builder threadNamePrefixExclude(String...values) {
327         threadNamePrefixExclude.clear();
328         if (values != null)
329            for (var v : values)
330               if (v != null && ! v.isEmpty())
331                  threadNamePrefixExclude.add(v);
332         return this;
333      }
334
335      /**
336       * Builds an {@link AdminProvider} instance.
337       *
338       * @return A configured instance.
339       */
340      public AdminProvider build() {
341         return new AdminProvider(this);
342      }
343   }
344}
